About ODIPA

Protecting Privacy
for Everyone

ODIPA is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to educating consumers, advocating for stronger privacy laws, and building open-source tools that protect people's digital rights.

Our Mission

Privacy is a fundamental right — not a luxury.

ODIPA was founded on the conviction that ordinary people deserve to understand and control their personal information — and that achieving this requires education, advocacy, and technology working together.

We deliver 77% of our services completely free to the public, reaching consumers, students, seniors, and community organizations who are most affected by data exploitation and have the fewest resources to protect themselves.

8
Active programs
77%
Services free to public
501(c)(3)
Tax-exempt status
2024
Year founded
Our Values

Privacy as a Right

We believe digital privacy is a fundamental human right, not a premium feature or a compliance checkbox.

Independence

ODIPA is beholden to no industry, government, or political party. Our positions reflect the evidence and the public interest.

Transparency

We publish our financials, our research methods, and our positions openly. Our code is open source.

Equity

Privacy protection should not depend on wealth or technical expertise. Our free programs serve all communities.

Collaboration

We work with individuals, businesses, government, and the global privacy community — not against them.

Evidence-Based

Our advocacy, research, and education are grounded in data, legal analysis, and demonstrated outcomes.

Our History
Nov 2024

ODIPA incorporated in California. Articles of incorporation filed November 14, 2024, establishing the organization's legal structure and charitable purpose.

2024–2025

Filed Form 1023 with the IRS for 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status. Established founding board of directors and began developing ODIPA's eight core programs.

Jan 2026

Received IRS 501(c)(3) determination letter confirming tax-exempt status.

2026

Launched odipa.org as a fully open-source website. Expanding to eight active programs, launching Corporate Certification, and growing national volunteer network.

Board of Directors

ODIPA is governed by an independent board of directors with expertise spanning privacy law, technology, nonprofit management, and community advocacy. Five seats are currently open — we are actively recruiting qualified candidates.

Current Board
JV
Jasper Vale
FOUNDING CHAIR
Chair, Board of Directors

VP of Software Development with 15+ years in enterprise software, banking technology, and privacy-first architecture. Founder of Avallis LLC and ODIPA's founding Board Chair.

Secretary & General Counsel

Treasurer

Director — Technology

Director — Community

Champions community engagement, volunteer programs, and equitable program access.

Ready to Get Involved?

Volunteer, donate, or partner with ODIPA to protect privacy for everyone.

DonateVolunteer
Organization for Digital Information Privacy & Awareness

Educating consumers and organizations about digital information privacy — advocating for practices and policies that protect people in an increasingly data-driven world.

ODIPA is funded through individual donations, corporate sponsorships, and foundation grants.

Privacy Newsletter

Privacy digest: breach alerts, new laws, research releases.

No spam, ever. Unsubscribe any time. We never sell your data. Privacy Policy
